title: Tangy Juicy Bhuna Beetroot Stir-fry
description: A tangy and juicy monsoon Argentinean dish featuring beetroot stir-fried with a blend of spicy and sweet flavors, ideal for a light meal.

ingredients.name: Beetroot
ingredients.notes: Ensure beetroot is cooked thoroughly to reduce oxalic acid.

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Fresh ginger adds a robust flavor and aroma.

ingredients.name: Tamarind Paste
ingredients.notes: For natural tartness and umami flavor.

ingredients.name: Cumin Powder
ingredients.notes: Adds a warm, earthy aroma and taste

ingredients.name: Red Chili Flakes or Crushed Red Pepper
ingredients.notes: For heat; adjust to personal preference.

ingredients.name: Low-Sodium Soy Sauce (or Tamari)
ingredients.notes: Enhances umami and adds a subtle sweetness.

ingredients.name: Lime Juice or Lemon Juice
ingredients.notes: Citrus juice brings out the natural flavors of beetroot and provides acidity.

ingredients.name: Vegetable Oil (or Olive Oil)
ingredients.notes: For sautéing vegetables and cooking the stir-fry

instructions: Wash beetroot thoroughly under cold running water. Peel it and cut into bite-sized pieces.
Heat a large pan or wok with vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Once the oil is hot, add chopped onions and sauté until translucent.
Add minced ginger, garlic, and cumin powder to the pan. Stir-fry for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Fold in finely chopped beetroot pieces. Sauté for another minute allowing them to absorb the spices.
Drizzle tamarind paste and red chili flakes into the pan. Mix well ensuring they are evenly distributed over the beetroot.
Stir in tomato sauce or paste, followed by lime juice (or lemon), soy sauce, and a pinch of salt. Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat to low.
Simmer for 5 minutes allowing flavors to meld together. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Once most of the liquid has been absorbed, turn off the heat. The dish should be slightly tangy and flavorful.
Plate the stir-fried beetroot with additional fresh lime wedges or a sprinkle of chopped cilantro (optional) for garnish.
